response.justification: The role centers on hands-on C++/STL development for high-performance industrial automation platforms with secure-development (IEC 62443) and IoT/protocol expertise. The candidate is a strong, experienced generalist with relevant distributed-systems, low-latency, and event-driven background, but C++ experience is minimal and dated (roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a university thesis). The primary stack is DLang, TypeScript/Node.js, and web/full-stack work, with no evidence of industrial cybersecurity, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, or SDL practices. Core technical fit for a C++-focused position is weak despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems and low-latency experience (microservices handling millions of requests/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software-craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and event-driven platform design
Broad multi-domain background across backend, embedded, and DevOps with proven ownership
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and over a decade old; not a primary language for the candidate
No industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443/SDL, or MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA protocol exposure
No industrial-automation domain experience and no Azure (cloud experience is AWS-based)
